# UC-22.1.46 · GDPR Consent Mechanism Audit (Lawful Basis Alignment)

## Description

By design, consent collection must be granular and verifiable. This use case monitors CMP and web telemetry for consent banner versions, non-essential cookies fired before consent, and mismatches between claimed purposes in the CMP and tags observed in the browser stream.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for GDPR, UK GDPR compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes consent, web and sourcetypes js_tag. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Instrument sites to emit tag and consent state events; (2) define required banner versions per site in policy lookup; (3) alert marketing and privacy when non-essential tags load pre-consent; (4) block releases that downgrade banner version without approval; (5) align with UC-22.1.5/UC-22.1.16 for withdrawal enforcement. Indexes required: `index=consent`, `index=web`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=js_tag`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: HTTP Event Collector (HEC), Splunk Add-on for Stream (Splunkbase 1809).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=consent` (CMP events), `index=web` (tag loading), `gdpr_consent_policy.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Instrument sites to emit tag and consent state events; (2) define required banner versions per site in policy lookup; (3) alert marketing and privacy when non-essential tags load pre-consent; (4) block releases that downgrade banner version without approval; (5) align with UC-22.1.5/UC-22.1.16 for withdrawal enforcement.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
(index=web sourcetype="js_tag") earliest=-24h
| eval before_consent=if(consent_state="unknown" AND match(tag_category,"(?i)marketing|analytics"),1,0)
| stats sum(before_consent) as early_nonessential_tags by site_id tag_vendor
| where early_nonessential_tags > 100
| lookup gdpr_consent_policy.csv site_id OUTPUT required_banner_version
| join site_id [
    search index=consent earliest=-24h
    | stats latest(banner_version) as live_banner by site_id
]
| where live_banner != required_banner_version
| table site_id, tag_vendor, early_nonessential_tags, live_banner, required_banner_version
```

Understanding this SPL

**GDPR Consent Mechanism Audit (Lawful Basis Alignment) (Art. 25(1), Art. 7)** — By design, consent collection must be granular and verifiable. This use case monitors CMP and web telemetry for consent banner versions, non-essential cookies fired before consent, and mismatches between claimed purposes in the CMP and tags observed in the browser stream.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=consent` (CMP events), `index=web` (tag loading), `gdpr_consent_policy.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): HTTP Event Collector (HEC), Splunk Add-on for Stream (Splunkbase 1809). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: web; **sourcetype**: js_tag. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=web, sourcetype="js_tag", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **before_consent** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates events by **site_id tag_vendor**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Filters the current rows with `where early_nonessential_tags > 100` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where live_banner != required_banner_version` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

See also the **CIM SPL** field in this use case; enable the matching data model acceleration if you run the tstats variant in production.

Output fields: before_consent, early_nonessential_tags, live_banner, required_banner_version, site_id, tag_vendor.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (sites with violations), Single value (sites off-policy), Heatmap (violations by region), Bar chart (tag vendors).

## SPL

```spl
(index=web sourcetype="js_tag") earliest=-24h
| eval before_consent=if(consent_state="unknown" AND match(tag_category,"(?i)marketing|analytics"),1,0)
| stats sum(before_consent) as early_nonessential_tags by site_id tag_vendor
| where early_nonessential_tags > 100
| lookup gdpr_consent_policy.csv site_id OUTPUT required_banner_version
| join site_id [
    search index=consent earliest=-24h
    | stats latest(banner_version) as live_banner by site_id
]
| where live_banner != required_banner_version
| table site_id, tag_vendor, early_nonessential_tags, live_banner, required_banner_version
```

## Visualization

Table (sites with violations), Single value (sites off-policy), Heatmap (violations by region), Bar chart (tag vendors). Key fields: `before_consent`, `early_nonessential_tags`, `live_banner`, `required_banner_version`, `site_id`, `tag_vendor`.
